meaning: n.嗡嗡声v.作嗡嗡声,嗡嗡作响,逼近
examples: I'll give you a buzz on Monday, OK?
Flying gives me a real buzz.
She gets a buzz out of her work.
The buzz on the streets is undeniably Latin American, of course, which creates an intoxicating blend.
Down in Soho, the buzz is all about the new David Burke Kitchen.
